boards.description: Interactive puzzle practice: the learner solves by manipulating the scene. One band per station; items are an ordered mission list. Luna narrates each prompt via `say`.

item.name: station
item.note: descriptive owner id; routing is by band

item.name: band
item.note: which station this mission belongs to

item.name: kind
item.note: mechanic id; selects the interactive component

item.name: say
item.note: narration key Luna speaks for the prompt (text must equal `prompt`)

item.name: prompt
item.example: Move one match to turn the 5 into a 3.

item.name: target
item.note: correctness target (matchstick: { goal: digits|equation|largest, accept?: string[] })

item.name: choices
item.note: pattern-pick only: the answer tiles (emoji strings)

content.description: Interactive puzzle missions for the practice board, partitioned by band (one band per station). Ordered: the host plays them in array order. Mechanic `matchstick`: numbers as seven-segment matchstick layouts; scene { start: "6+0=9", moves: 1..2 }; target goals — `digits` (final digits must be in `accept`), `equation` (ANY arithmetically true equation counts), `largest` (must equal the BFS-computed maximum reachable number, so every optimal solution counts and no accept list is authored). Mechanic `shape-count` (Shape Shore): scene { vertices: [[x,y]…] (integer coords), edges: [[i,j]…], find: triangles|squares|rectangles }; target { mode: choose|claim }. The answer key is DERIVED from the figure by src/practice/shapeGeometry.js (composite shapes whose sides span collinear edges are found automatically) — authors never write counts, so keys can't be wrong; narration -ok lines that mention a number must match the derived count (verify with the geometry module before baking voice). Mechanic `pattern-pick` (Pattern Peaks): scene { layout: sequence|matrix|odd, cells: [emoji strings, '?' = the hole] }; top-level `choices` (emoji list; omit for odd) and target { answer: <choice> } or { index: n } (odd layout — tap the cell directly). A cell may hold several emoji (growing patterns like 🔵🔵🔵). Riddle Rapids mechanics — `river-cross`: scene { cast: [{id,emoji,label}], forbidden: [[idA,idB]…] }, target { allAcross: true } (boat = farmer + 1; leaving a forbidden pair alone resets the trip). `pour-jugs`: scene { jugs: [caps…] }, target { amount } (fill/empty/pour; built-in BFS knows the optimum and fires the hint after ~2× that many actions). `which-fills`: scene { segs: [{id,x1,y1,x2,y2, to:[ids]|cup:id}], blocked: [segIds], cups: [{id,x}] }, target { predict: true } — the winner is DERIVED by flooding the graph (design puzzles so exactly ONE cup is reachable); kid predicts, water animates and proves it. Logic Lagoon mechanics — `balance`: scene { scales: [{left,right,heavier:L|R}] (facts as tilted see-saws), items: [{id,emoji,label}] }, target { ask: heaviest|lightest, id }. `logic-grid`: scene { owners, items (both [{id,emoji,label}]), clues: [{a: ownerId, rel: is|not, b: itemId}] }, target { assign: {ownerId: itemId} } — author MUST verify the clues force the assignment uniquely. `sudoku`: scene { symbols: [4 emoji], solution: [16 x 1..4], given: [16 x 0|1] }, target { complete: true } — verify the given-mask yields a UNIQUE solution with a solution-counting script before shipping.
